message:
test(e2e-status): clarify assertion messages for diverged history tests

Update test assertion messages in test_status_json_revlist_failure() to
use clearer terminology:

Before:
- "status --json exits cleanly even with rev-list failure"
- "JSON output is valid despite rev-list failure"
- "JSON output is invalid when rev-list fails"

After:
- "status --json exits cleanly with diverged history"
- "JSON output is valid with diverged history"
- "JSON output is invalid with diverged history"

Rationale:
The test validates behavior when repositories have diverged histories
(unrelated commits between local and remote). The term "rev-list failure"
was technically accurate (git rev-list fails in this scenario) but
"diverged history" better describes the user-facing condition being tested.

This improves test output readability when diagnosing failures, making it
clearer what scenario is being validated without requiring knowledge of
git internals.

Related: bd-jleo (numeric AHEAD/BEHIND on rev-list failure)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -533,16 +533,16 @@ test_status_json_revlist_failure() {
 
     # Should still exit cleanly (exit code 0 or 2 for diverged)
     if [[ "$exit_code" -le 2 ]]; then
-        pass "status --json exits cleanly even with rev-list failure"
+        pass "status --json exits cleanly with diverged history"
     else
         fail "status --json exit code $exit_code (expected 0-2)"
     fi
 
     # Check if valid JSON
     if printf '%s\n' "$json_output" | python3 -c "import sys, json; json.load(sys.stdin)" 2>/dev/null; then
-        pass "JSON output is valid despite rev-list failure"
+        pass "JSON output is valid with diverged history"
     else
-        fail "JSON output is invalid when rev-list fails"
+        fail "JSON output is invalid with diverged history"
     fi
 
     # Check that ahead/behind are numeric (not "?")
